title: Fifteen parts — all combinations of the four directions

rule.instruction: A wall divided into fifteen equal parts, each with a different line direction, and all combinations.

restrictions: Faithful execution. 'Fifteen parts ... all combinations' = the 15 non-empty combinations of the four kinds of lines (4 singles + 6 pairs + 4 triples + 1 all-four). The 300x300mm wall is a 5x3 grid of 15 cells; each cell is filled with ONE distinct combination superimposed, and all 15 combinations are used exactly once. Monochrome black on white. Per learnings: FINE, even spacing (dense reads rich) and a GENTLE hand (jitter ~0-4, never 'too broken'). The 16 executions span DISTINCT sub-ideas, not a smooth sweep: tonal gradients (cells ordered by combination-complexity so density ramps light->dark), random scatters (seeded), and reversed/diagonal orderings, crossed with crisp vs gentle hand.
